title: Stopping Conditions
description: Learn when decision trees stop splitting and how to control overfitting.
takeaways: Trees stop when nodes are pure or reach a depth limit.
max_depth, min_samples_split, and min_samples_leaf control growth.

practiceLab.objective: Demonstrate max_depth, min_samples_split, min_samples_leaf, pure-node stopping, and constrained growth on Iris.
instructions: Run the Iris loading cell and confirm 150 samples and 3 classes.
Run the max_depth comparison for values 1, 3, and None.
Run the min_samples_split comparison for 2, 20, and 50.
Run the min_samples_leaf comparison for 1, 10, and 30.
Complete Exercise 1 on a pure toy dataset and confirm tree depth is 0.
Complete Exercise 2 comparing an unconstrained tree with a constrained tree (max_depth=2, min_samples_leaf=10).
Record how leaf counts change under constraints.
Answer the reflection questions about which rule reduced complexity most.
hints: model.get_depth() and model.get_n_leaves() summarize tree size.
A pure label vector should produce depth 0.
Tighter min_samples_* values generally create fewer leaves.
practiceLab.expectedOutput: Printed depth/leaf summaries for max_depth, min_samples_split, and min_samples_leaf sweeps; pure-node depth 0; unconstrained vs constrained depth/leaf comparison.
